author: @AndreaDingbatt
text:
AH HA!! ~YOU, HAVE MY HUBBY!!
I was just wondering where he'd Escaped to!!!
Please look after him, he's a *bit* Prone to Electrical Accidents, so Do Not Let him Change the Light bulbs!!
(* He Once Used a Hammer to try to 'Change' the Oil Filter on his Mini!!)
You are soo welcome to Teach him Stuff, but Stand Well Back!!
Make sure that you are properly insured....
Do Not Let Him Cook, and you'll be great,....
Just, don't feed him after Midnight, nor, must you Ever buy him a Ticket back to Carlisle,..
.Or I will be on the way down to stay with You!!🙂😅
(* With Pigeons!!)
Much love and hugs, 
Andrea and Critter Family. XxX

Had the Teachers at the Schools

That there wouldn't be any climate crisis...!!
A. Dingbatt. Mrs. XxX.

author: @jvon3885
text: Eh they are disabling the dislike buttons on everything now so they won't show up for anyone e to see except the content creator. Guess constructive criticism isn't needed anymore.

replies.author: @jvon3885
replies.text: @roblastname9133 it just depends on the content provided. It allows creators to understand their audience and the audience to know if it's worth the time. I have limited hours and I tend to use it as a way to scrub out videos that might take up valuable time. And sadly we all know that A. The algorithms add a fraction of a dislike to every like to keep creators in check and B. Trolls will abuse this button. So some have to be taken with a grain of salt, over all my comment was just a jab at sensitive types who can't handle any form of criticism and not directed towards anyone in particular.

replies.author: @jvon3885
replies.text: I do however wish they would just add the function that if you want to leave a dislike you are forced to describe your reasoning. They can't just leave the like button because that becomes misleading. It's all political anyways.
